svg.font-family: -apple-system, Segoe UI, Roboto, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif
svg.aria-label: How the Mythos NSA 'breach' claim narrowed from June 11 testimony to the June 24 Project Glasswing disclosure
svg.title: The Mythos / NSA claim, narrowed: from "broke into classified systems" to "identified vulnerabilities under Glasswing"
svg.text: Each step down is a weaker, better-sourced version of the same event. Width ≈ strength of the assertion.
Jun 11 · Sen. Warner, relaying Gen. Rudd (NSA/CyberCom)
“Mythos broke into almost all of our classified systems — not in weeks, but in hours.”
“found vulnerabilities,” against test environments — not a live production breach.
Jun 24 · AP, citing a US official + Project Glasswing
tested against replicas of classified environments under Glasswing red-teaming.
identified & chained flaws in hours — not necessarily exploited.
"identify a vulnerability in hours" ≠ "exploit it in hours" ≠ "breach a live system" — the gap the headline elided.
Glasswing is Anthropic's defensive offensive-security program (covered Jun-08 §7, Jun-11 §3). New on Jun-24: the NSA test ran *inside* it.
Why it matters: the export-ban's stated basis (Jun-12 BIS order) rests on the strongest version of this claim, now materially softened.
Sources: AP / News4Jax, Business Standard, Reuters (Jun 24, 2026). Earlier framing: Jun-21 brief §1.
